content.thinking: The cluster is healthy with 3 nodes. The /app vhost exists and app-queue is a classic queue with no policy applied. I need to create an ha-all policy on the /app vhost that will configure classic queue mirroring across all nodes.

content.text: The cluster is healthy, `/app` vhost and `app-queue` exist, but no policy is set. I'll now set the `ha-all` policy using `rabbitmqctl set_policy`.

input.command:
NS="karma-rabbitmq-failover-first-01-2026-d7245467-cluster-a"
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl set_policy ha-all ".*" '{"ha-mode":"all","ha-sync-mode":"automatic"}' --vhost /app --apply-to queues
input.description: Set ha-all mirroring policy on /app vhost

content.content: Setting policy "ha-all" for pattern ".*" to "{"ha-mode":"all","ha-sync-mode":"automatic"}" with priority "0" for vhost "/app" ...

tool_use_result.stdout: Setting policy "ha-all" for pattern ".*" to "{"ha-mode":"all","ha-sync-mode":"automatic"}" with priority "0" for vhost "/app" ...

input.command:
NS="karma-rabbitmq-failover-first-01-2026-d7245467-cluster-a"
echo "=== Policies on /app ==="
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_policies -p /app
echo ""
echo "=== Queues on /app ==="
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_queues -p /app name type policy slave_pids synchronised_slave_pids
echo ""
echo "=== Cluster status ==="
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl cluster_status --formatter=compact
